c74object.name: fluid.kdtree~
c74object.digest: Efficient lookup of data using a k-d tree.
c74object.description: A k-dimensional tree for efficient neighbourhood searches of multi-dimensional data.

discussion.p: fluid.kdtree~ facilitates efficient nearest neighbour searches of multi-dimensional data stored in a fluid.dataset~.

k-d trees are most useful for repeated querying of a dataset, because there is a cost associated with building them. If you just need to do a single lookup then using the kNearest message of fluid.dataset~ will probably be quicker

Whilst k-d trees can offer very good performance relative to naïve search algorithms, they suffer from something called “the curse of dimensionality” (like many algorithms for multi-dimensional data). In practice, this means that as the number of dimensions of your data goes up, the relative performance gains of a k-d tree go down.
Read more about fluid.kdtree~ on the learn platform.
fluid.kdtree~ is part of the Fluid Decomposition Toolkit of the FluCoMa project. For more explanations, learning material, and discussions on its musicianly uses, visit flucoma.org.

description.p: Name for this instance, so that it can be reused or referred to by other data objects. If none is given, a name will be generated

method.name: reset
method.digest: Return the object to its original state
method.description: Calling reset will change parameter values back to those the object was created with, i.e. any attribute values set in the box will be retained

method.name: refer
method.description: Changes the reference to the data in another named fluid.kdtree~ object. Changes to the data stored in any referenced will be shared by all other similarly named fluid.kdtree~ objects.

description.p: Build the tree by scanning the points of a fluid.dataset~

ul.li:
dataSet:
The fluid.dataset~ of interest. This can either be a data set object itself, or the name of one.

description.p: Returns the identifiers of the k points nearest to the one passed.

ul.li:
buffer:
A buffer~ containing a data point to match against. The number of frames in the buffer must match the dimensionality of the fluid.dataset~ the tree was fitted to.

k:
(optional) The number of nearest neighbours to return. The identifiers will be sorted, beginning with the nearest.

description.p: Get the distances of the K nearest neighbours to a point.

ul.li:
buffer:
A buffer~ containing a data point to match against. The number of frames in the buffer must match the dimensionality of the fluid.dataset~ the tree was fitted to.

k:
(optional) The number of nearest neighbours to return. The identifiers will be sorted, beginning with the nearest.

description.p: The number of columns (dimensions) in this model or dataset / labeset
Resets the internal state of the model
The number of data points (entries / observations) in this model or dataset / labeset

method.name: load
method.description: Replace the internal state of the object from a dict. The message should take the form load dictionary <dictid>

method.name: dump
method.digest: Dump the state of this object from the dump outlet as a dict.

method.name: write
method.description: Save the internal state of the object to a JSON file on disk. With no argument, a file dialog will appear to choose the file. It is always executed at the lowest priority on the main thread, using deferlow internally.

method.name: read
method.description: Replace the internal state of the object from a JSON file on disk. With no argument, a file dialog will appear to choose the file. It is always executed at the lowest priority on the main thread, using deferlow internally.

description.p: The number of neighbours to return.

The maximum distance (in high dimensional space) that a returned point can be. Any points beyond radius will not be returned (even if they're within the nearest numneighbours points). When radius is 0, it is no longer a constraint and the distance of a nearest neighbour is not taken into account.
Enable warnings to be issued whenever a parameter value is constrained (e.g. clipped)
